commands.review_summary:
The PR adds provider-owned tool-schema normalization cache keys, caches normalized tool parameters in the embedded-agent runner, adds an `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` kill switch, updates provider SDK exports/docs/API baseline, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +289, Tests +470, Docs +12, Generated 0. Total +771 across 16 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a performance/API PR rather than a bug report. Source and focused tests show the cache mechanics, but they do not establish real embedded-agent/provider behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human handling is needed because the remaining blockers are contributor real-runtime proof and maintainer acceptance of a new provider SDK/env contract, even though the docs mismatch is mechanically fixable.

target.title: perf(agents): cache provider tool schema normalization
target.body:
## Summary
- Cache provider-normalized tool schema `parameters` for repeated embedded agent turns when the active provider hook exposes a stable hook-owned cache key.
- Keep the cache conservative: the key includes provider hook identity plus provider/model/runtime inputs and ordered original tool schema signatures; cached values clone only tool `parameters`, not tool objects, execute closures, approval state, or guards.
- Add `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` as a kill switch, SDK docs/API surface for `resolveToolSchemaCacheKey`, and focused cache-key, clone-safety, env-disable, eviction, and runtime-handle coverage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated embedded agent turns no longer redo provider tool-schema normalization when the provider hook identity, provider/model/config inputs, and ordered tool schema signature are unchanged. Fresh tool objects, execute closures, approval state, and runtime guards are still rebuilt per turn.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `tool-schema-cache-pr3`, current head `d892395657`, rebased onto latest `origin/main` at `4dad7bd93b`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Rebuilt the branch from current `origin/main`, regenerated the plugin SDK API baseline, ran the focused schema/runtime tests, core test typecheck, formatter and diff checks, and `pnpm build`.

Evidence after fix:

```text
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts --reporter=dot
3 Vitest shards passed: 1 file / 11 tests, 3 files / 234 tests, 1 file / 55 tests

node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
exit 0

./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 docs/plugins/architecture-internals.md docs/plugins/sdk-provider-plugins.md src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/runtime-plan/build.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/plugins/provider-hook-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/types.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check
exit 0

pnpm build
exit 0; phase timings total 132.0s, slowest tsdown 83.9s
```

Observed result after fix: the provider schema-normalization cache stores normalized parameters once and reuses cloned parameters on subsequent matching calls while preserving fresh tool closures. Regression coverage also proves cache keys are resolved from the same prepared provider runtime handle used for normalization, preventing cross-plugin reuse.

What was not tested: full live-provider end-to-end latency was not tested. This PR caches only provider-normalized schema `parameters`; it does not cache complete tool inventory/materialization, policy resolution, prompt inputs, or full agent-turn startup.
